data.narrative:
Asylon Robotics is a Series B-stage autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $40M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 7 private peers in the same sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero; no public comparables exist in this narrowly defined category.

The stage-progression method is applicable and yields a $76M implied valuation, using a 1.9x step-up multiplier applied to the $40M total raised proxy to reflect typical venture capital markup from Series B forward. This reflects expected dilution and capital efficiency expectations at this stage. The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient data density—while four same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) produced the minimum three datapoints required for robust median calculation. A cross-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the specificity of autonomous systems valuations.

Based on the single applicable method triangulated with conventional stage progression uncertainty bands, the analysis supports a valuation range of $65M (low) to $87M (high), with a median point estimate of $76M. The primary uncertainty is the absence of same-stage peer multiples; resolution would require either additional Series B-stage comparable data or disclosure of metrics (revenue, burn rate, technical milestones) that could anchor alternative valuation approaches.
